content.problemStatement:
You are given an integer kkk. You want to choose nnn points on the plane and join some pairs of those points with line segments (let us call them edges), such that:


n<510n < 510n<510
Any two points are distinct
No three points are collinear
The points have non-negative integer coordinates ‚Č§109\leq 10^9‚Č§109.
No two edges (drawn line segments) intersect at a point which is not an endpoint of either edge.
The number of triangles in the figure is exactly kkk.
**Note:** A triangle means a triple of points such that there is an edge between any two of them.

It is guaranteed that it is possible to select a set of points and line segments satisfying the above conditions.


Input Format

The only line of the input contains kkk, the number of required triangles.


Output Format

In the first line, print two integers, nnn and mmm, the number of chosen points and the number of line segments (edges) drawn between them. They must satisfy 1‚Č§n<5101 \leq n < 5101‚Č§n<510 and 0‚Č§m‚Č§n(n‚ąí1)20 \leq m \leq \frac{n(n-1)}{2}0‚Č§m‚Č§2n(n‚ąí1)‚Äč.
The ithi^{\text{th}}ith of the next nnn lines should contain two space-separated integers, xi,yix_i, y_ixi‚Äč,yi‚Äč, denoting the ithi^{\text{th}}ith chosen point. The coordinates must be non-negative and ‚Č§109\leq 10^9‚Č§109, i.e., 0‚Č§xi,yi‚Č§1090 \leq x_i, y_i \leq 10^90‚Č§xi‚Äč,yi‚Äč‚Č§109.
The ithi^{\text{th}}ith of the next mmm lines should contain two integers uiu_iui‚Äč and viv_ivi‚Äč, denoting a line segment connecting the uithu_i^{\text{th}}uith‚Äč chosen point and the vithv_i^{\text{th}}vith‚Äč chosen point. 1‚Č§ui,vi‚Č§n1 \leq u_i, v_i \leq n1‚Č§ui‚Äč,vi‚Äč‚Č§n should be satisfied. No edge should be printed more than once.

If there are multiple solutions, you can print any of them.

Explanation:
The figure shows the configuration corresponding to the given output. It contains 4 points and 5 edges. Clearly, there are 2 triangles, as the input requires it to be.
